101.title: Geralt of Rivia
101.extract: Geralt of Rivia, known as the White Wolf, is a witcher: a mutated, professionally trained monster slayer for hire. Geralt carries two swords, silver for monsters and steel for men.

102.title: Yennefer of Vengerberg
102.extract: Yennefer of Vengerberg is a powerful sorceress born on Belleteyn. Yennefer is famed for her raven-black hair, lilac eyes, and her stormy bond with a certain witcher.

103.title: Ciri
103.extract: Ciri, princess of Cintra, is the Lady of Time and Space, carrier of the Elder Blood. Ciri was raised at Kaer Morhen and trained by witchers.

104.title: Triss Merigold
104.extract: Triss Merigold of Maribor is a sorceress and healer known as the Fourteenth of the Hill. Triss is a close friend of the witchers of Kaer Morhen.

105.title: Dandelion
105.extract: Dandelion is a celebrated bard, poet, and professor whose ballads chronicle the adventures of his best friend, a famous witcher.

106.title: Vesemir
106.extract: Vesemir is the oldest and most experienced witcher of the Wolf School, serving as fencing instructor and father figure at Kaer Morhen.

107.title: Zoltan Chivay
107.extract: Zoltan Chivay is a dwarven warrior and trader, a loyal drinking companion famed for his colorful parrot and generous heart.

108.title: Emhyr var Emreis
108.extract: Emhyr var Emreis is the Emperor of Nilfgaard, called the White Flame Dancing on the Barrows of his Enemies.

109.title: Philippa Eilhart
109.extract: Philippa Eilhart is an ambitious sorceress and advisor to kings, a founding member of the Lodge of Sorceresses who can take owl form.

110.title: Regis
110.extract: Regis is a higher vampire and barber-surgeon, an erudite and gentle soul with a fondness for mandrake hooch.

111.title: Lambert
111.extract: Lambert is a sharp-tongued witcher of the Wolf School, the youngest of the Kaer Morhen witchers and famously bad at politeness.

112.title: Eskel
112.extract: Eskel is a calm and dependable witcher of the Wolf School, bearing a facial scar and a reputation for level-headedness.
